posts.caption:
America might not do royal weddings, but the upcoming nuptials between NFL star Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift, now the biggest female artist in history, is about as close as they can get.
Ever since the couple announced their engagement last August, the internet has been awash with rumours about the big day. Where will it be? When? Who will Taylor wear? Is Stevie Nicks actually going to perform?
All signs (with particular thanks to New York City Mayor, Zohran Mamdani) suggest the Swift-Kelce wedding will be a multi-day celebration taking place in NYC this weekend. Madison Square Garden, a 22,000-person capacity arena used for both music and sporting events (hint, hint), is heavily tipped to be the venue of choice. A venue of that scale (and security of that calibre) can only mean one thing: it’s going to be the wedding of the century.
May the rumour mill keep turning all week - which ones do you believe?

posts.caption:
Fashion has no shortage of beautiful faces, but @alexconsani represents a new generation of supermodels—the ones not just walking the runway but creating platforms, building communities, and leading with personality. First gaining attention on TikTok, Consani is now one of the world’s most sought after models. “I’m having fun,” says Consani. “I’m doing it for all 50 of the other bitches that would love to be in that room.” For Bazaar’s Summer 2026 issue, the supermodel talks about trying her hand at acting, moving into her big-girl home, and entering her “experimental era.”
-

The Prince of Wales attended the three-year anniversary of the Homewards Programme at the Tate Modern, reflecting on the initiative’s progress in tackling homelessness and driving long-term change. 🔗Link in bio for all the latest details

Taron Egerton at Wimbledon giving the face we all do when we think no one’s watching 😳 Or is this just him channelling his APEX mindset… “thought you liked danger?” 👀 Either way, Centre Court has never had better reactions.

We stepped right into the heart of the action with Emirates, the official airline partner of The Championships, for exclusive behind-the-scenes access on Day 2 at Wimbledon 🎾✨
Spotted soaking it all in: David Gandy, Vanessa Williams and Nicky Hilton, bringing effortless glamour as the matches unfolded. 🔗Tap the link in bio for all the details. @emirates

Caroline Flack’s brother, Paul Flack, has died at the age of 55. 💔 The news comes more than six years after the loss of the much-loved TV presenter. Our thoughts are with the Flack family and all those who knew and loved him during this difficult time. 🔗 Link in bio for all the details

posts.alt_text:
If there’s one thing we’ve learnt about @dellfrance, it’s that they know how to subvert the expectations of tech launches. 

Last Friday, Dell Technologies launched the new XPS 13 at Paris Fashion Week with HAUS, offering a sanctuary of music and technology to escape from the Paris heatwave. Kicking off on Thursday evening with the launch event, the Friday all-day programme featured an unmissable lineup curated by @rinse.fm.

With performances by @preciousreneetucker, @mikelikesrap, and @soundsignature, HAUS expanded beyond the tech launch party mold, and became a cultural fixture within the PFW program.

📸: @adrienozouf

posts.caption:
@camper x @isseymiyakeofficial just made the case for shoes that wear like socks. ⁠
⁠
Dubbed the Anna, the sculptural new silhouette hugs the foot like a second skin, pairing easy slip-on comfort with the kind of thoughtful design that's become synonymous with Issey Miyake.⁠
⁠
The structured outsole makes it perfect for everyday wear and versatility, no matter the occasion. Expect the shoe to be released on July 15.
posts.alt_text:
While many already know that @skechers played a huge part in helping superstar @oanunoby on his journey to an @nba championship, what they might not know is that “São Paulo” by @theweeknd was just as essential in keeping him locked in.

We caught up with OG at the Skechers showroom following his championship-winning season to talk about the sneakers and soundtrack that fuel one of the Knicks’ biggest stars.

posts.caption:
For thousands of football-obsessed kids, summer means traveling to Gothenburg to compete in the @GothiaCup_Official, the world’s biggest youth football tournament. This year, @skfgroup is making the trip even more memorable with a new Meet the World jersey designed by Swedish football stars Monica Jusu Bah and Felicia Schröder. Inspired by matching sun-and-moon tattoos the pair got to celebrate their friendship, the kit tells the story of connection on and off the pitch.⁠
Find out more by hitting the link in bio.
Ellos juegan por la copa, nosotras por el handbag ranking ⚽✨ Y chicas... The bags did not disappoint! Cuál es tu favorito? #WorldCup 2026

posts.alt_text:
Fashion is finding creative freedom in the last place you’d expect… @onlyfans. With algorithms deciding what gets shared (or hidden), fashion is having to think more carefully than ever about what gets shown. Brands have become experts at gaming the feed, but have they also started designing for it? Welcome to the age of algorithmic modesty.

For those still wanting their creative freedom, OF delivers it. Despite its association with adult content, the platform has quietly become one of fashion’s most unexpected creative playgrounds. @rickowensonline, @poster__girl__official, @collinastrada and @louisgabrielnouchi are just some of the designers using the space to share their work beyond the limits of traditional social media.

With OnlyFans, an audience arrives intentionally rather than stumbling across content mid-scroll. This is part of a wider shift away from the open web and towards gated ecosystems and self-selecting communities. Writers are launching Substacks, brands are building Discord channels and designers are experimenting with Patreon-style memberships. OFTV feels less like a social app and more like a streaming service where fashion can exist on its own terms.

The future of fashion online may not be one giant feed shared by everyone. Instead, it may consist of smaller, opt-in worlds where designers speak directly to audiences willing to follow them there.

As fashion moves away from platforms that dictate taste and towards spaces where creators control the narrative, virality is no longer everything. Access might be the new luxury.

So, if you’re really into fashion in 2026, you might need to make yourself an OnlyFans account.

Images: Courtesy of brands, @thecobrasnake, @karlatomanelli

Another off-season show has come and gone for @marcjacobs, but we’re still stuck backstage floored by the beauty. For the Spring 2027 collection, @marcjacobsbeauty played a key part in celebrating the brand’s love for bold color and playful yet powerful looks. We’ll be scrolling through this BTS from @thealexandraarnold all week. 😮‍💨
Images: Courtesy of Marc Jacobs, Alexandra Arnold
posts.alt_text:
From Nickelodeon star to beauty co-founder, @victoriajustice’s career is powered by passion projects. After being diagnosed with an autoimmune disorder called Hashimoto’s, the actor left behind traditional perfumes in pursuit of something cleaner. Thankfully, she discovered @nakedperfume’s refined scents and joined the brand to further its mission. 🌸 Now, she’s dishing her top 5 beauty products with Hypebae, from the NAKED perfume that started it all to the makeup essentials she’s been using since middle school. Hit the link in bio to read the full interview.

Video: Victoria Justice

posts.caption:
@jordynhuitema is one of the most recognizable faces in women’s soccer. An Olympic gold medalist, one of @canwnt’s brightest talents and an @nwsl stalwart, the 25-year-old star has an impressive list of accolades to her name already. This year she’s adding one more to the list: skincare maven.
Entering the market as the first skincare line designed with athletes in mind, Huitema’s @jordyskin is a revelation in the world of beauty. Despite so many people identifying as athletes, no brand has come up with a formula made specifically for them. Huitema took the matter into her own hands, and now she’s sharing her secret to a healthy skin barrier with the world.
We talked to Huitema about the upcoming JORDYSKIN launch and her latest career move. Head to the link in bio to read the full conversation.
Images: Jordyn Huitema

posts.alt_text:
How does @johnglacier get in the right state before performing? A song on loop and a stiff drink. ⁠
⁠
The UK artist breaks down her pre-show essentials for our June @hypetrak digital cover — solitude, Jamaican rum, cranberry juice, a splash of cognac, the right ring, and her Prada bag. ⁠
⁠
The routine's minimal — "a bit of a bore," she admits. The loud part comes after the show, and it's the only real essential: a burger.⁠
⁠
Read the full cover story via the link in bio. ⁠
⁠

On the brink of her second album, Lack of Joy Is Death, the London rapper, writer, and producer makes the case for joy as radical resistance: “You can’t kill the stories, you can’t kill the dancing; you can’t kill the joy, you can’t kill the culture.”

posts.caption:
Marseille turned into VIRGINIA for the day.⁠
⁠
In partnership with @adidasoriginals and @pharrell, Boa Vista Beach Club became a creative playground where sport, music and community collided. Locals, friends and family came together for beach volleyball, capture the flag, paddle relay races and a full day of programming before the shoreline transformed at sunset with a set from Kaytranada.⁠
⁠
Built around Pharrell's creative platform, VIRGINIA is more than a place. It's a mindset rooted in culture, collaboration and creative expression, brought to life through music, design, live experiences and community.⁠
⁠
Tap in for a look inside the beach takeover in Marseille.

posts.caption:
According to @lab____off____white “If Off-White™ defines the grey area between black and white, L/AB is another shade within that spectrum, a new space for experimentation, evolution and play.”⁠
⁠
While Abloh’s “Off-White For All” label has been retired, this new arm of the brand is meant to do the same thing: to act as an accessible entry point geared towards young consumers. As reported by WWD, the re-emergence of the Off-White sublabel will be accompanied by campaigns with the likes of JT, Glaive and PZ; models Vivian Jenna Wilson, Julez Smith and Mazzy Joya, and content creator Jay Guapo.⁠
⁠
Check out some of the standout pieces above and stay tuned for the official drop!⁠
⁠
Photo: Off-White L/AB c/o⁠

posts.caption:
Summer doesn’t arrive all at once. It unfolds in moods, moments, and memories. ☀️🌿
@ahsaassy_ steps into the season’s softest chapter, where easy silhouettes and unhurried afternoons become a reminder that slowing down is its own kind of luxury.
In collaboration with @aurelia_womenswear , this explores the evolving moods of summer, from nature’s quiet embrace to the intensity of the afternoon sun, and the effortless cool of golden evenings.✨
This is a celebration of the season in all its shades, beginning with the calm before the heat, where style isn’t about standing out, but feeling completely at home in your own skin.
Team Credits:

Summer doesn’t arrive all at once. It unfolds in moods, moments, and memories. ☀️🌿

@ahsaassy_ steps into the season’s softest chapter, where easy silhouettes and unhurried afternoons become a reminder that slowing down is its own kind of luxury.

In collaboration with @aurelia_womenswear , this explores the evolving moods of summer, from nature’s quiet embrace to the intensity of the afternoon sun, and the effortless cool of golden evenings.✨

This is a celebration of the season in all its shades, beginning with the calm before the heat, where style isn’t about standing out, but feeling completely at home in your own skin.

posts.caption:
@jennifer.garner *always* knows what to do (and it usually involves sorghum).
Between starring in summer’s buzziest new series, taking her near-billion dollar company public, and turning ‘Pretend Cooking Show’ into a reality, she still manages to be the nicest woman in Hollywood.
Playing a widowed food blogger in @Peacock’s adaptation of @elinhilderbrand’s bestseller ‘The Five-Star Weekend,’ the beloved actress and entrepreneur channels her inner Ina Garten for a not-so-Instagrammable girls’ weekend on Nantucket with co-stars-turned-IRL besties, @chloesevigny, @morereginahall, @gemmachan and @darcycarden, exploring what it means to grow and develop in middle age.
At 54, the Marvel Hero and supermom reflects on her genre-spanning career, being “total besties” with John Cena, her surprisingly vast knowledge of weaponry, plus the responsibility of celebrities to stand up for working locally for InStyle’s Summer Imagemakers Issue at the link in @instylemagazine’s bio now.

posts.caption:
From an "arts and crafts at home" project to becoming one of the most exciting brands behind wedding attire, it's safe to say the designers behind @tanner.fletcher were not expecting this.
While many find the bow-covered tuxedos and quirky vintage-inspired wedding gowns synonymous with #TannerFletcher, the story behind how Tanner Richie and Fletcher Kasell's wedding line became a success was totally unexpected: "We just totally winged it. Now, it's 50% of the business." For the latest installment of #BehindTheDesign, @lady_hadeline spoke to the designers behind the brand about everything from antiquing to planning their own wedding.

posts.caption:
There’s no duality in the way Babil Khan (@babil.i.k) exists; what you see is what you get. A bundle of energy and a force of conviction, under the spotlight, but especially outside of it. In fact, now there’s an added sheen of alignment, and it looks great on him.
From opening up about navigating the industry to finally finding his voice, our latest cover star unpacks the family legacy and identity.
Head to the link in bio to read the entire cover story.
Editor: Analita Seth (@analitaseth)
Photographer: Chandrahas Prabhu (@chandrahas_prabhu)
Styling: Who Wore What When (@who_wore_what_when)
Assisted by: Shubham J (@d.shubham_j)
Interviewed by: Sushri Sahu (@sushri_sahu)
Makeup: Mohak Chavan (@muamohak) and Aniket Kamble (@aniket_cz)
Hair by: Tej (@hairby_tej)
Artist Reputation Management: Hardly Anonymous Communications (@hardlyanonymouspr)
Jacket & Pants - NM Studio (@nm_design_studio)
Shirt - Rare Rabbit - (@rarerabbit_in)
Shoes - Ecco (@ecco)
Sunglasses - Marc Jacobs (@marcjacobs)
[LSA Cover Star, Babil Khan, Bollywood, Cover Story, July 2026]
posts.alt_text:
Anuv Jain’s (@anuvjain) music is what feelings sound like. Love, passion, calm, longing, and so much more… Words may fail, but you’ll always find an Anuv Jain song, or a lyric that resonates with you. This is the power of music, and what Anuv’s rise has always been about.

Be it Husn, Baarishein, Alag Aasmaan, or his latest, Arz Kiya, Anuv’s music has had a chokehold on the youth and has given words to emotions felt by crowds.

He claims to be a yearner, someone who likes to live in the past at times, feels too deeply, and expresses himself with intention. This Music Day, we raise a toast to his music, his success, and his love for the craft.

Editor: Analita Seth (@analitaseth)
Photographer: Sasha Jairam (@sashajairam)
Fashion Editor: Neelangana Vasudeva (@neelangana)
Produced by: Chirrag Guha (@guha.archie)
Interviewed by: Tanzim Pardiwalla (@pepper_tan) 
Junior Stylist: Karnika Kabra (@karnika_kabra) 
Hair and Makeup by: Chriselle Ann Baptista (@chrissybaps)
Assisted by: Pinky Roy (@pinkyroy9467)
Artist Reputation Management: Aurum Entertainment (@aurum_entpr) 

Wardrobe partner: Pepe Jeans (@pepejeansindia)

Accessories from @ishhaara via @ascend.rohank 

[LSA Cover Star, Anuv Jain, Music, Musician, June 2026]

posts.caption:
Sharvari (@sharvari) is poised for stardom. She is sharp yet soft, fierce yet deeply grounded. Committed to her craft with a candour that feels increasingly rare, “Without sounding smug, I really want to be known as the best actor of my generation.”
And she’s well on her way.
For our May cover, Sharvari sheds all inhibitions while shining unapologetically under the spotlight.
Read the cover story in the link in bio.
Editor: Analita Seth (@analitaseth)

posts.caption:
@TravisScott è stato difficile da rintracciare. E non senza motivo: il rapper cresciuto a Houston è stato impegnato a viaggiare in giro per il mondo oppure chiuso nel suo studio di Malibù a lavorare al seguito di #Utopia, il suo album del 2023. Ha trascorso due anni in tournée in tutto il mondo con il #CircusMaximusTour. Tra un concerto e l’altro, ha girato due film: a luglio apparirà nei panni di un bardo tra il cast stellare di #TheOdyssey di Christopher Nolan, mentre a ottobre interpreterà se stesso nella commedia “Rolling Loud” (basata sull’omonimo festival). Senza contare la collaborazione quasi decennale con @Nike e il suo ruolo di chief visionary di @Oakley. Nel frattempo ha continuato a gestire #CactusJack, il suo collettivo creativo e la sua etichetta discografica, che ha messo sotto contratto artisti come Don Toliver e Sheck Wes. Continua a leggere l'intervista a cura di @BrittanySpanos al link in bio e su @LOfficielItalia, Luglio 2026.
Global Chief Content Officer @giampietrobaudo⁠
Photography @gregswalesart
Styled by @luca_falcioni_

posts.alt_text:
Un rovescio a una mano, raro nel tennis di oggi, e un gioco elegante e variato: @Lore_Musetti, 24 anni, ha aperto il 2026 conquistando il quinto posto del ranking ATP, prima che un infortunio alla gamba sinistra lo costringesse a rinunciare al Roland Garros. Global ambassador di #BottegaVeneta, il tennista si racconta a @LOfficielItalia, ritratto dagli scatti di @RaffGrosso e con lo styling di @Fernando.Echeverria_, vestendo un total look della collezione #Fall2026, firmato dalla direttrice creativa @Louise_Trotter_. Tra scaramanzie da spogliatoio, il bronzo olimpico di Parigi, l'ammirazione per il tennis di Alcaraz e la passione per la cucina: «Colpire la pallina, il suono che fa quando la colpisco: è questo che mi rende felice». Leggi l’intervista a cura di @Fabia.DiDrusco al link in bio e sul nuovo numero de @LOfficielItalia, ora in edicola.

posts.caption:
Introducing our global cover star, @TravisScott!
The Houston-born artist has spent the past year moving between worlds, crafting the follow-up to 2023’s ‘Utopia’ from his Malibu studio while preparing for his much-anticipated acting debut as a bard in Christopher Nolan’s @theodysseymovie, in theaters July 17.
From music to film to fashion, Scott’s creative universe continues to expand. He also stars alongside Owen Wilson in the upcoming comedy ‘Rolling Loud’ and maintains long-running partnerships with @Nike and @Oakley, all while leading @cactusjack into its next chapter.
Yet at the center of it all remains the music. “I’ve been mad inspired over the last year and a half,” Scott says. “I’ve been buckled down and back building beats from scratch, just creating and pushing the sound.”
Read our full cover story at the link in bio as Scott opens up about the creative forces that drive him, his big-screen debut, and the importance of self-care.

posts.caption:
The version of @travisscott the public sees is only part of the story.
While he spends much of his time focused on family and “self-preserving,” Scott says the energy people associate with him is genuine. “I love to dance,” he says. “On stage is not different from when I’m actually outside, listening to music or in a club. People think I want to break laptops. No, no, no. Who doesn’t break a drink here or there?”
For Scott, balance comes from slowing down. “I do spend a lot of time at home. I spend a lot of time with my kids, taking things in and working within,” he says. “That’s peaceful for me. I sit; I cook. Then when I do pop out, I let the energy flow.” Read more from Scott at the link in our bio.

posts.alt_text:
The 2026 July/August issue is here!

At the center of this year’s Summer Entertainment Issue is @TravisScott, who opens up about working on the follow-up to 2023’s ‘Utopia,’ the travels and experiences fueling his next chapter, and making his acting debut as part of a star-studded ensemble in Christopher Nolan’s ‘The Odyssey.’ 

As much as we love a blockbuster with a stacked A-list cast, championing fresh talent is always top of mind. Inside the issue, we spotlight eight rising stars whose work this summer demands your attention, from ‘Outer Banks’ star @MadisonBaileyBabe to Avantika of ‘Not Suitable for Work’ to @sashacalle of ‘Sugar.’ 

It’s also a blockbuster time for literary escapes, as the rise of #BookTok turns reading into an unexpected status symbol that’s shaping summer travel. Looking ahead to the Fall collections, we explore the age-old question of whether sex still sells, given the state of undress on the runway, red carpet, and social feeds in 2026. Stay tuned for the issue hitting newsstands this month.

posts.caption:
The 2026 July/August issue is here!
At the center of this year’s Summer Entertainment Issue is @TravisScott, who opens up about working on the follow-up to 2023’s ‘Utopia,’ the travels and experiences fueling his next chapter, and making his acting debut as part of a star-studded ensemble in Christopher Nolan’s ‘The Odyssey.’
As much as we love a blockbuster with a stacked A-list cast, championing fresh talent is always top of mind. Inside the issue, we spotlight eight rising stars whose work this summer demands your attention, from ‘Outer Banks’ star @MadisonBaileyBabe to Avantika of ‘Not Suitable for Work’ to @sashacalle of ‘Sugar.’
It’s also a blockbuster time for literary escapes, as the rise of #BookTok turns reading into an unexpected status symbol that’s shaping summer travel. Looking ahead to the Fall collections, we explore the age-old question of whether sex still sells, given the state of undress on the runway, red carpet, and social feeds in 2026. Stay tuned for the issue hitting newsstands this month.
Editor-in-Chief of @lofficielusa @caroline_grosso⁠

#TravisScott
posts.alt_text:
The version of @travisscott the public sees is only part of the story.

While he spends much of his time focused on family and “self-preserving,” Scott says the energy people associate with him is genuine. “I love to dance,” he says. “On stage is not different from when I’m actually outside, listening to music or in a club. People think I want to break laptops. No, no, no. Who doesn’t break a drink here or there?” 

For Scott, balance comes from slowing down. “I do spend a lot of time at home. I spend a lot of time with my kids, taking things in and working within,” he says. “That’s peaceful for me. I sit; I cook. Then when I do pop out, I let the energy flow.” Read more from Scott at the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
Introducing our global cover star, @TravisScott!
The Houston-born artist has spent the past year moving between worlds, crafting the follow-up to 2023’s ‘Utopia’ from his Malibu studio while preparing for his much-anticipated acting debut as a bard in Christopher Nolan’s @theodysseymovie, in theaters July 17.
From music to film to fashion, Scott’s creative universe continues to expand. He also stars alongside Owen Wilson in the upcoming comedy ‘Rolling Loud’ and maintains long-running partnerships with @Nike and @Oakley, all while leading @cactusjack into its next chapter.
Yet at the center of it all remains the music. “I’ve been mad inspired over the last year and a half,” Scott says. “I’ve been buckled down and back building beats from scratch, just creating and pushing the sound.”
Read our full cover story at the link in bio as Scott opens up about the creative forces that drive him, his big-screen debut, and the importance of self-care.
Global Chief Content Officer @giampietrobaudo

posts.alt_text:
@TravisScott è stato difficile da rintracciare. E non senza motivo: il rapper cresciuto a Houston è stato impegnato a viaggiare in giro per il mondo oppure chiuso nel suo studio di Malibù a lavorare al seguito di #Utopia, il suo album del 2023. Ha trascorso due anni in tournée in tutto il mondo con il #CircusMaximusTour. Tra un concerto e l’altro, ha girato due film: a luglio apparirà nei panni di un bardo tra il cast stellare di #TheOdyssey di Christopher Nolan, mentre a ottobre interpreterà se stesso nella commedia “Rolling Loud” (basata sull’omonimo festival). Senza contare la collaborazione quasi decennale con @Nike e il suo ruolo di chief visionary di @Oakley. Nel frattempo ha continuato a gestire #CactusJack, il suo collettivo creativo e la sua etichetta discografica, che ha messo sotto contratto artisti come Don Toliver e Sheck Wes. Continua a leggere l'intervista a cura di @BrittanySpanos al link in bio e su @LOfficielItalia, Luglio 2026. 

Global Chief Content Officer @giampietrobaudo⁠

posts.caption:
Film has long been part of his creative language, but for @TravisScott, acting is the next frontier. The artist, who directed many of his own visuals early in his career, makes his big-screen acting debut in Christopher Nolan’s @theodysseymovie this July, joining a star-studded cast that includes @Zendaya, @TomHolland2013, and Matt Damon.
A reimagining of Homer’s ‘Odyssey’ set in the aftermath of the Trojan War, the film marks Scott’s second collaboration with Nolan, following his contribution of the end-credits track “The Plan” to ‘Tenet’ in 2020. This time, Scott steps in front of the camera as a bard—a role that gave him a newfound appreciation for the filmmaking process.
“I commend everybody that does it, from the grips to the lighting to all those people that go off on taking it to the next level,” he says. “The whole cast, it’s like one family, you know what I’m saying? A group of people who were all on one mission to deliver. To be adopted into that family felt very cool.”
Read more about Scott’s acting debut, his creative evolution, and the pursuits shaping his next chapter at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
After more than a decade in the music industry, @TravisScott is no stranger to evolution. Following the success of 2023’s ‘Utopia’ and a two-year global tour, he’s already focused on the next era of his sound, returning to his Malibu studio to build beats from the ground up. Although he keeps details of the new project closely guarded, he does open up about the travel, experiences, and moments that continue to fuel both his creativity and ambition.
“I’m traveling around and seeing so many different things. It might just be something you see on the street or the different type of aura you feel driving through a different country.” Even as his world continues to expand, Scott says the fundamentals of his creative process remain the same—he just knows himself better now. “A lot of the things that I was trying to figure out when I was younger, I know now,” he tells L’OFFICIEL. “And I’m just pushing myself to get better.”
Visit the link in bio to read our full interview with Scott.

posts.caption:
If summer had a dress code, this would be it. @jacquemus presents ‘Le Bonheur,’ a Spring/SUmmer 2027 collection inspired by the carefree joy of summer and the beauty of the outdoors. Set along the path to the Phare de la Pietra in Corsica, it celebrates sunshine, simplicity, and the feeling of being completely at ease. “I imagined this collection like the summer itself. The sun, the colors, the beauty of simple things, the feeling of lightness. For me, this is happiness... le bonheur,” @simon_porte_jacquemus said.
Blending the house’s signature sculptural silhouettes with playful minimalism, ‘Le Bonheur’ is filled with fluid movement, vibrant color, and thoughtful craftsmanship. Delicate organza techniques, paper-thin leather, cascading ostrich feathers, and reimagined sporty essentials elevate everyday pieces into wearable works of art. A painterly palette of soft pastels contrasted with bold pops of yellow, green, orange, and aquamarine creates a collection that feels fresh, effortless, and unmistakably summer.
Photos courtesy of Jacquemus
#jacquemus #simonportejacquemus

posts.caption:
@Balenciaga dropped its Spring Summer 2026 preview collection in Mumbai last night, and the energy in the room felt different.
With Fatima Sana Sheikh, Bobby Deol, Mouni Roy, Sanya Malhotra, Malavika Mohanan, and Krithi Shetty in attendance, it felt less like a formal showcase and more like a real moment around fashion. The room was sharp, observant, and engaged.
Fatima Sana Shaikh said it best: “Balenciaga always has a chic approach and the brand is now heading towards it even more aggressively.” And that tone carried through the evening. Nothing felt loud for attention, yet everything commanded it.
What stood out was how people responded. There was curiosity, closer inspection, and genuine interest in the details. Some silhouettes felt familiar but more refined, while others brought a quiet sense of drama without trying too hard.
That is when a collection works.
Not when it demands attention, but when it earns it.
@balenciaga #Balenciaga

posts.caption:
Luxury isn’t just about what you own. It’s about access to experiences and opportunities that remain out of reach for most.
From private islands and bespoke travel to rare collectibles, exclusive real estate, and invitation-only circles, extreme wealth unlocks a lifestyle built around privacy, personalization, and exclusivity. While some of these luxuries have always been symbols of status, others have become even more desirable as demand for unique experiences continues to grow.
Here are some of the things that, even today, remain reserved for the world’s wealthiest.

Valentino Beauty has announced Dakota Johnson and Alexander Skarsgård as its newest global ambassadors.
The duo will front the house’s upcoming global fragrance campaign, bringing together Hollywood talent and Valentino’s modern vision of beauty. The appointment reflects the brand’s continued focus on storytelling, individuality, and timeless elegance as it strengthens its presence in the global luxury beauty market.

posts.caption:
Formula 1 drivers aren’t just defined by speed. Many have signature fragrances that reflect their personalities away from the racetrack.
From fresh and understated scents to bold luxury perfumes, today’s F1 stars have embraced fragrance as part of their personal style, proving that grooming has become just as important as fashion off the grid. Whether arriving in the paddock or attending global events, their choice of scent has become another expression of identity.
Here are the fragrances worn by some of Formula 1’s biggest names.

posts.caption:
#ManifestCover
There is a quiet power to Rukmini Vasanth (@rukmini_vasanth). One that doesn’t seek attention, yet holds it effortlessly.
For Manifest’s May–June cover, she steps into an imaginative world where temple jewellery meets contemporary silhouettes, blurring the lines between memory and modernity. She embodies a woman in transition, grounded, luminous, and yet constantly becoming.
“I’m not very good at describing myself,” she says, almost in passing.
Yet, in the same breath, her choices reveal a quiet clarity of intent. “I don’t want to isolate my character. I need to understand the whole world it exists in.”
Between restraint and reinvention, stillness and movement, she exists in that rare in-between. Not fully defined, not confined. Just on the cusp of a wider sky.
Our latest issue is now live on Magzter. Read the full cover story from the link in bio.

posts.alt_text:
They got each other at hello! A four-hour FaceTime call blossomed into a friendship and a budding romance, and he proposed at a gorgeous Italian villa, no less!

That led Chanpreet Garcha (@_cgarcha), an optometrist, and Jasnayak (Jay) Sohi, a commercial investment advisor and insurance entrepreneur, to plan a beautiful wedding in Los Cabos. It could well have been straight out of a travel map, filled with adventure and family bonding-from a sunset soirée welcome overlooking the sea to a pickleball match and their traditional Anand Karaj ceremony in a mango orchard!

In a sense, their celebrations also paid nod to something they both love-travel!

Read via link in bio 🔗 
By Ismat Tahseen (@ismat.tahseen)

📷: Amrit Photography (@amritphotography)

posts.caption:
Mercury retrograde is basically the universe’s “maintenance mode.” It’s the astrological phase when Mercury—the planet associated with communication, travel, technology, and thinking—appears to move backwards in the sky. (It isn’t actually reversing direction; it’s just an optical illusion caused by the way Earth and Mercury orbit the Sun.)
In astrology, this backward motion is believed to bring a little extra chaos to the things Mercury rules. Science doesn’t support these effects, but many people use the period as a reminder to slow down, double-check details, and revisit unfinished business.
[Astrology, Horoscope, July 2026, Zodiac Signs]

posts.caption:
For the longest time, men’s jewellery began and ended with a watch, a wedding band, or maybe a classic pair of cufflinks. But that’s evolving, bracelets have quietly become one of menswear’s coolest accessories.
The key is to keep it simple. Wear one with your favourite watch, stack a couple together, or add a little sparkle with diamonds.
We’ve rounded up the most minimalist and bejewelled bracelets every modern gentleman should have on his radar.
[Mens Fashion and Jewellery, Gifting, Gold and Silver]
posts.alt_text:
#ManifestCover 

There is a quiet power to Rukmini Vasanth (@rukmini_vasanth). One that doesn’t seek attention, yet holds it effortlessly.

For Manifest’s May–June cover, she steps into an imaginative world where temple jewellery meets contemporary silhouettes, blurring the lines between memory and modernity. She embodies a woman in transition, grounded, luminous, and yet constantly becoming.

“I’m not very good at describing myself,” she says, almost in passing.

Yet, in the same breath, her choices reveal a quiet clarity of intent. “I don’t want to isolate my character. I need to understand the whole world it exists in.”

Between restraint and reinvention, stillness and movement, she exists in that rare in-between. Not fully defined, not confined. Just on the cusp of a wider sky.

Our latest issue is now live on Magzter. Read the full cover story from the link in bio.

Chairperson: Avarna Jain (@avarna_jain)
CEO: Debashish Ghosh
Editor: Aindrila Mitra (@aindrilamitra) 

Interview: Akshay Kaushal (@akshay_kaushal) 
Photography: Vaishnav Praveen (@vaishnavpraveen)
Styling: Mohit Rai (@mohitrai)
Fashion assistant: Shubhi Kumar (@shubhi.kumar)
Hair: Rohit Bhatkar (@rohit_bhatkar)
Makeup: Riviera Lynn (@rivieralynn)
Video: Kris Arshad (@itskrisarshad) 
PR Agency: Tree-Shul Media (@treeshulmediasolutions)
Artist management: Benchmark Talents (@benchmarktalents)
Production: Studio Little Dumpling (@studiolittledumpling)

On Rukmini: pantsuit, Kanika Goyal Label (@kanikagoyallabel); earrings, bangles, and rings, Gehna Jewellers (@gehnajewellers1)

An @rpsg.lifestylemedia publication by @rpsggroup

posts.caption:
In India, silver has never been just another wedding gift. It has always symbolised prosperity, abundance and good fortune, making it one of the most meaningful ways to bless a couple as they begin a new journey.
From handcrafted tea sets, pooja essentials to artisanal tableware and contemporary home décor, silver continues to becoming part of wedding gifts—one that holds emotion, value and legacy.
Manifest spoke to India’s leading silverware design houses that are reimagining centuries-old craftsmanship for modern homes.
Read more via the link in bio 🔗
By Srishti Mukherjee (@sri.sshhhhh)
[Wedding Gift Ideas 2026, Bride and Groom, Gifting Ideas, Indian Silverware, Luxury, Heirloom]

posts.caption:
The story of how Lord Ganesha became the god of all things goes far beyond mythology.
Mythologist Devdutt Pattanaik (@devduttmyth) traces Ganesha’s origins back to the elephant—a symbol of wealth, fertility, power and opportunities in ancient India. Over centuries, that symbolism evolved into the elephant-headed deity we know today, representing not just prosperity, but also contentment, balance and auspicious beginnings.
Read more via the link in bio. 🔗
[Ancient History, Ritual and Traditions, God and Goddess, Indian Culture, Spirituality, Mythology, Ganesha, Ganpati]

posts.caption:
For the last several days, our fashion reporter Jacob Gallagher has been in Paris for the men’s fashion shows. This season, there were coastal palm prints at Louis Vuitton, fan-enhanced tracksuits at Rick Owens and disco pants at Dior. Willy Chavarria delivered optimistic American sportswear, and Celine’s collection was risky, but rich with ideas.
At the link in our bio, read what @jacobwgallagher thought about shows in Paris. And subscribe to The Fashions newsletter to get dispatches from more runways straight to your inbox. Photos by Adrien Dirand, OWENSCORP, Tom Nicholson/Reuters, Dior Homme, Simon Wohlfahrt/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images, Soshiotsuki, Celine, Yannis Vlamos
posts.alt_text:
Marc Jacobs’s spring 2027 show, held at the New York Public Library, was the first runway show the designer had held after LVMH, the world's largest luxury group, sold his brand to WHP Global. Was it, as speculation had gone, his last show? Was it, rather, proof that nothing much would really change?

The show was fast and colorful as usual. It featured chromatic, transparent layers in cellophane, vinyl and sheer knits, mixed and matched for all to see. Belts were clear plastic and dripping with chains, necklaces were loaded up and chunky and many looks came without any pants at all.

"It served as a reminder that when Jacobs wants, he can stick his thumb in the air and figure out which way the wind is blowing," wrote Vanessa Friedman, chief fashion critic for The New York Times. "And make some really good stuff."

At the link in our bio, read @vvfriedman's full review and see more looks from the @marcjacobs show. Photos by Alexandra Arnold, Marc Jacobs

posts.caption:
Telling tales from the ancient past of 1990s, the comedian Guy Branum is teaching Gen Z what their forefathers knew.
@guybranum’s video series “What the Old Gays Remember,” is a highlight reel from before 2000, when gay marriage was barely talked about, when few celebrities were out, and when drag queens had not yet sashayed their way into prime time.
With his videos, Branum is showcasing a different moment, and what it meant to claim those slivers of culture as his own. He tells campy stories like the time Celine Dion upstaged Aretha Franklin (“like an idiot,” he said) at VH1’s “Divas Live” in 1998. But he also reflects in earnest on the bodybuilding star Bob Paris, who gave up his career in 1989 by coming out of the closet.
He has embraced the role of teacher but “this is not journalism,” he told The New York Times. “This is history told to you through the telephone.” At the link in our bio, read more about how Branum is imparting his knowledge to young gays. Photos by @michellegroskopf

posts.caption:
After the premiere of “The Invite,” a snappy comedy about a tense marriage that stars Olivia Wilde, Rogen, Cruz and Edward Norton, a buzzy crowd gathered at the Chateau Marmont in West Hollywood to celebrate.
The party was a victory tour for Wilde’s third major directorial effort, which she said was partly influenced by her high-profile relationships and breakups. Speaking on the red carpet, Wilde said she wanted to explore the idea of “taking responsibility for your own happiness and, within relationships, never getting to a place where you’ve lost your identity.”
At the party, Cruz and her husband, Javier Bardem, mingled with fellow guests like Dakota Fanning, Kesha and Rashida Jones, who wrote “The Invite” with Will McCormack. On the patio, guests enjoyed signature cocktails and charcuterie while Seth Rogen and his wife shared a poolside joint. At the link in our bio, get an inside look at the after-party and read more about the film's premiere. Photos by @alishajucevic

posts.caption:
One of my favourite details from #Jacquemus’ sun-soaked show in Corsica was the series of headscarves that became part of the garments themselves. These looks embodied @simon_porte_jacquemus’ vision of Mediterranean dressing.
A close-fitting hood was tied at the nape of the neck before flowing down the back as a long panel of fabric, draping into a cowl seamlessly transforming into a dress or coat. It was a sculptural silhouette that feels both minimalist and dramatic, relying on fluid construction.
They also recall the practical headscarves traditionally worn by women across the #Mediterranean, the draped silhouettes of ancient #Greek and #Roman dress, and veils seen in religious imagery.
posts.alt_text:
For Spring 2024, #MiucciaPrada and #RafSimons created a series of #Prada dresses made from “Super Organza,” featuring layers of fabric that drifted behind the models as they walked. The lightweight, ultra-fine variant of organza was developed by Japan’s Amaike LLC. Approximately one-fifth the thickness of a human hair, the fibre is so delicate that it can be cut by static electricity, making it exceptionally fragile and challenging to weave into fabric. Weighing only around 5 grams per square metre, #SuperOrganza is considered one of the lightest fabrics in the world.

The dress gained further prominence when #HunterSchafer wore a custom version to the 2024 Golden Globe Awards (styled by @dara._ ) solidifying its place among the most theatrical and technically innovative silhouettes of the year.

posts.caption:
#JonathanAnderson’s latest #Dior Mens collection was a masterclass in craftsmanship. What may look like ordinary printed textiles were objects of exceptional couture craftsmanship.
Look 20, a black and white spotted coat, from a distance, appeared to be a simple polka-dot (stingray?) fabric, yet on closer inspection revealed a painstakingly hand embroidered textile created from thousands of individually applied beads.

The trompe-l’œil bow shirts embroidered by @lesage were inspired by a 1979 #HauteCouture dress by #MarcBohan, reimagining heritage for a modern menswear audience.

Corsages were also reinterpreted by @blanka_flavin into intricately hand beaded and metal cast floral brooches.

posts.caption:
What better way to cool down than a trip down memory lane to the #Chanel Spring 2018 waterfalls?
#KarlLagerfeld drew inspiration from the Gorges du Verdon in southern France and enlisted @atelierdevineau to create a cliff stretching approximately 85 metres across and rising around 15 metres high inside the Grand Palais. The set was complete with caves from which models emerged, while multiple functioning waterfalls poured down.

posts.caption:
There’s nothing better than a cohesive dialogue between mens and womenswear, and #SarahBurton’s debut #Givenchy menswear collection translates the visual language she’s established in her women’s collections into a wardrobe for the Givenchy gentleman.
Look closely and you’ll spot familiar details, tailored suiting is softened by lush floral embroideries from the viral spring 2026 blooms, gemstone cufflinks nod to the chandelier top, and playful metal figurine pendants recreate miniature runway looks.
For the first #menswear presentation since 2024, Sarah Burton ushers in a new era creating a house identity that feels thoughtful, modern, and unmistakably cohesive.

posts.caption:
After almost five years in Paris, #AlexanderMcQueen has announced that it will be returning home, bringing its Spring/Summer 2027 show back to #LondonFashionWeek this September.
A true homecoming, some of Lee’s most extraordinary runway finales were created in #London, the city where his vision first captivated the fashion world. An emotional climax of the story, His shows often ended with moments that blurred the boundaries between fashion, theatre, performance art and technology, leaving audiences stunned and creating images that are still discussed decades later.

posts.caption:
Justin Bieber for Issue 01: “The First Ever” Outlander Magazine In-Print!
This is a real piece of history for us all. The past 10 years of lessons and learning have led up to this moment and I couldn’t be happier to share it with you all.
Some of you may be new followers who discovered us last week and some of you may have been here since day 1, either way this is for everyone who aligns with what it means to be an outlander.
Also, this is a great time to introduce myself for the first time in 10 years. My name is @callum.mccaff & we are all Outlander. I was born in 1996 so we priced the magazine at £19.96.
Available worldwide at www.outlandermag.com
PRINT ISN’T DEAD.
🫂 @lilbieber
📸 @sweetcian
🔗 @freenjoy
posts.alt_text:
Naomi Osaka for Outlander Magazine wearing her custom Nike Roland Garros looks designed by Nike & Kevin Germanier!

Roland Garros’ best dressed just raised the bar again. Naomi’s custom Nike looks were designed by Kevin Germanier, the couture designer turning upcycled materials into something iconic. Clay court season needed a moment like this, so we took her to an authentic orange clay court in paris to match the energy she’ll be surrounded by this week😤🤝

posts.caption:
Travis Scott for Issue 01: “The First Ever” Outlander Magazine In-Print!
GANG, TRAVIS SCOTT IN HERMÈS FOR OUR FIRST EVER MAGAZINE COVER. THIS IS UNHEARD OF, AND BETTER YET? IT WAS FOR OUTLANDER MAGAZINE. I AM HUMBLED.
WOW, what a moment. My only wish in this life is that what I’ve spent the last 10 years building acts as a strong form of inspiration for your own creative careers - I came from no fashion background, I had no connections & I had no idea what I was doing, but, I did it anyway.
If you didn’t know by now, my name is @callum.mccaff , I founded Outlander - and I’m here to remind everyone that PRINT ISN’T DEAD.
Available worldwide at www.outlandermag.com

posts.caption:
India's Renee Noronha has won first place in the 18-24 category at the Ironman Philippines. Completing the gruelling challenge at 15:55:37, the 20-year-old IIT Madras student upheld held her record of being the youngest Indian female Ironman finisher.
Renee battled torrential rains and unforgiving temperatures to complete the race. In conversation with SheThePeople, the triathlete also revealed that she participated on day 3 of her period, making the victory a profound reminder of her strength and resilience as a woman.
With this win, Renee has now qualified for the Ironman World Championship Hawaii, scheduled for October 2026. She is also on a mission to set a new Guinness world record to become the youngest person in the world to do a full Ironman on 6 continents. She is on track to achieve this by April 2027, at the age of 21.
Read full interview at www.shethepeople.tv. | @renee.noronha
#Ironman #Philippines #sports full Ironman challenge, women in sports, women in endurance sports, mumbai sports, Chennai sports, IIT Madras student, women in STEM, ironman Kona, triathlon, marathon, menstrual awareness

posts.caption:
Imagine you’re in a hurry to leave for work and you tried fixing your hair for the fifth time still it doesn’t appear how you want it to be. Will that not ruin your entire day? It sounds relatable right? This is called ‘mirror effect’. The 'Mirror; shows our reflection but the ‘Effect’ influences how we think and feel about ourselves.
We think a mirror is something that shows our face but it acts more than that. It’s a feedback tool; you don’t just see yourself but you start to think and react emotionally and make quick judgements. The mirror acts as a tool of self-criticism, self-appreciation and comparison because mirror interactions shape how we feel about ourselves.
Visit www.shethepeople.tv to read more
#mirror #insecurity self-criticism, mirrors, reflection, negativity, self esteem, appreciation, confidence, mood swings, stress, judgements, viral, trends, explore

posts.caption:
Imagine spending the most precious times with someone, thinking about them all the time, and weaving desires of marriage, only to have them declare someone else as the love of their life one day. What was supposed to be the best time of your life becomes the worst. Social media calls this a "placeholder partner."
A placeholder partner is someone good enough to date and spend years with, but never the person who gets pursued for a long-term relationship or marriage. Their partners believe in the ideology: "Let's have fun with the wrong ones while you wait for the right one." The term has made every person in a relationship ask, "Am I a soulmate to them, or just a proxy for someone else?"
A placeholder partner is involved in a seemingly normal relationship. It includes daily chats, trips, meeting families, never getting tired of calling each other's names, showing affection in public, and making promises of forever. In reality, it's an illusion of togetherness, a temporality.
Visit www.shethepeople.tv to read the full article by @_shivanilamba
#love #relationships long-term relationships, placeholder partner, Kareena Kapoor, Jab we met, break-up, couple goals, bollywood, marriage, dating, chat, commitments, soulmates, desires, viral, trending

posts.caption:
Everybody knows Dadasaheb Phalke, the creative legend behind India’s first full-length silent feature film, Raja Harishchandra. He is widely known as the Father of Indian Cinema.
But there was someone, without whom, his films would not have come to life. That was his wife, Saraswatibai Phalke, believed to be India’s first ever film editor and technician.
Saraswatibai Phalke, also known as Kaveribai Karandikar, was 14 years old when she married Dadasaheb Phalke in 1902. After their marriage, Saraswatibai learned technology and provided complete support to her husband in filmmaking.
Although she was not as popular as Dadasaheb, her contribution to Indian film-making is beyond valuable.
Visit www.shethepeople.tv to read more

posts.caption:
There’s a way to plan a wedding where one event is the highlight and the rest are filler.
And then there’s the way Noopur and Mehul’s wedding was planned - where every single function felt like the main event. A Haldi as vibrant as the celebration that followed it. A Jaggo night that refused to let the energy dip. A grand wedding day. And a reception that closed it all out exactly the way it deserved to be closed.
No filler. Just four unforgettable days, back to back.
And then there was them. ❤️
But what made this celebration truly stand out was how consistently it delivered - nothing front-loaded, nothing left to coast through. Every function built on the last, holding its own weight, right down to a final look in deep emerald velvet that closed the celebration with the same grandeur it opened with.
Some weddings have one moment everyone remembers.
And some give you four - each one as considered, as elevated, and as unforgettable as the last. 🌟
Couple: @noopur_ahluwalia & @mehul_jethva
Venue: The Oberoi Sukhvilas, Chandigarh
Wedding Planner: @shaandaarevents
Photos Courtesy: @sunnydhimanphotography
#JustChekhmateIt

posts.caption:
Jaipur doesn’t do weddings quietly.
It does them with forts standing guard over the festivities, with mountains framing a Mehendi like they were always meant to be the backdrop, and with a grandeur that doesn’t need to be manufactured - it’s simply built into the city’s bones. Every function here carries weight before a single decoration goes up.
Tarisha and Prerit’s wedding understood that completely.
And then there was them. ❤️
But what made this celebration truly royal at every turn was how seamlessly each event built on the last - a Mehendi overlooking the mountains, a grand Baraat that announced itself the way only a Jaipur Baraat can, and a Sangeet that never let the energy dip. Thoughtfully planned from the very first function to the very last.
Some weddings impress you with one unforgettable moment.
And some stay with you because every single function held its own - elegant, memorable, and unmistakably fit for a fort. 🌟
Bride: @tarishatholia
Groom: @preritkothari
Venue: Jaigarh Fort, Jaipur (Wedding) | Kothari Garh, Jaipur (Sangeet) | Jai Mahal Palace, Jaipur (Mehendi)
Planner: @everafterweddingsindia & @royaloccasionsjaipur
Photos Courtesy: @raabta.studios
#JustChekhmateIt
Celebrations Simplified

posts.caption:
Fashion styling is a job. Not a hobby. Not a passion project. Not something people do on the side because they love clothes.
It is a profession that takes years to build. The eye. The contacts. The knowledge of how the industry actually works across every sector, every brief, every type of client. That does not come from loving fashion. It comes from years of showing up, doing the work and figuring it out mostly alone.
And yet it is still one of the most undervalued roles in the creative industry.
Stylists are asked to work for free. For credit. For exposure. For a rate that does not come close to reflecting the skill, the hours or the experience involved. And somewhere along the way the industry decided that was normal. That stylists should be grateful for the opportunity. That loving what you do means you should not expect to be paid properly for it.
We are past that.
Behind every iconic image, every red carpet moment, every campaign that stopped you mid-scroll, there was a stylist. Building the looks, interpreting the brief, managing the pressure, absorbing the chaos so nobody else had to. Often without a credit. Sometimes without a proper rate.
That changes when stylists know their worth, charge it without apology and stop accepting the jobs that treat their career like a favour.
➡️➡️ If you are a working fashion stylist who is ready to do exactly that, comment STUDIO and we will send you the free trial link to The Studio of Stylist Elixir. Rate guides. Contracts. Portfolio tools. Masterclasses every Monday. Everything you need to build a career that reflects what you are actually worth.
Save this. Send it to a stylist who needs to hear it.
posts.alt_text:
Jonathan Anderson just showed his first couture collection for Dior. If you do not understand why it mattered, this post is for you.

John Galliano was front row. The artisans were the opening act. The silhouettes came from ceramicist Magdalene Odundo.

If you are in Paris: the show space at Musée Rodin opens to the public from 28 January for one week. The new couture collection, archival Dior pieces, and the Odundo ceramics that inspired it. You can see it yourself.

This is not about liking or not liking a collection. This is about understanding what you are looking at.
The Studio of Stylist Elixir is our online membership. Guides. Templates. Live calls. A community of stylists who are learning and building together. Comment ‘studio’ for a free trial.

Save this. Share it with someone who needs it.

#dior #jonathananderson #stylistelixir

posts.caption:
Somewhere along the way, fashion stylists learned to apologise for the job.
Sorry the rate is that. Sorry the prep takes days. Sorry, could I ask the budget. Sorry to chase the invoice. Sorry, I can’t do this one for exposure.
The rate is the rate. The prep is the work. The budget question is professional, not rude. The invoice is owed, not a favour. And exposure has never once paid rent.
This is for the stylist who softens the number before anyone has even pushed back. For the one who throws in the prep day to seem easy to work with. For the assistant learning, right now, that the sorry is optional.
It is. Put it down.
Save this for the next time you go to quote, and send it to the stylist who still apologises.
Comment STYLIST to be in a room with stylists who charge like they mean it.
With love, Em x 💚

posts.caption:
People think this job is shopping. It is the last five percent of what we do.
The effortless looks take the most work. The rate is the eye, not the hours. Sourcing is half the job and the glamour is about ten percent of it. And exposure has never once paid an invoice.
What is the one thing people always get wrong about what you do? Tell me below, and tag a stylist who feels this.
Day 20 of 30 🤍

posts.caption:
After enough years you can spot each of these within five minutes.
The one who says « effortless » and means three hours of work. The one with forty Pinterest screenshots on forty different bodies. And every so often, the one who starts as a job and ends up family.
Send this to the stylist who would get it. They have met every one too.
Now the real talk. Knowing your clients is easy. Booking better ones is a different skill, and it is the one no one ever taught us.
That is what The Studio is. It is my membership for working stylists, the room I wish I had when I started. Inside you get the rate breakdown from over a thousand stylists so you stop guessing your worth, the pitch templates that actually get replies, masterclasses on portfolio, sourcing and client work, a new live event every month, and a hundred plus stylists on hand when you are stuck. It is everything between having the eye and actually being booked for it.
Right now it is £20 a month, cancel anytime. In August it becomes The BOOKED Method and the price more than doubles, and everyone already inside keeps the £20. Comment STUDIO or tap the link in my bio.
Day 18 of 30 🤍
#fashionstylist #personalstylist #fashionstyling #stylistlife #clientstories

posts.caption:
For a country obsessed with real estate, surprisingly little attention is paid to why every expensive home now looks like it could exist anywhere.
Somewhere along the way, modern Indian architecture began to mistake global sameness for aspiration.
The irony is that traditional Indian architecture already understood the weather. Jaalis, courtyards, bamboo, mud and stone were not decorative flourishes. They were engineering.
Now, concrete boxes are cooled with air conditioners and sold as luxury.
The future of Indian architecture may lie in remembering that sustainable architecture in India existed long before the phrase became fashionable. The smartest Indian house design may be the one that learns from local materials instead of replacing them.
Read more at the link in bio.
By Aarushi Agrawal (@aarushi_agrawal01)
#IndianArchitecture #Architecture #SustainableDesign #ClimateAction
posts.alt_text:
Until sometime back, beauty was the only goldmine for investor funding and acquisitions. Recently, wellness has been giving some steep competition.

Supplement brand The Func. Lab (@thefunclab), co-founded by fitness instructor Sohrab Khushrushah (@sohrabk82), cricketer-turned-entrepreneur Sahil Kukreja (@sahilkukreja99), and luxury brand builder Daneesh Davar (@daneeshd) in 2025, becomes the latest name on the block to raise a substantial investment within a year of beginning operations.

In its seed round, the nutrition start-up has successfully secured an amount of $1.5 million, powered by a group of well-established names such as Nisaba Godrej, Anand Piramal, Bhakti Modi, and Abhishek Nayar, among others.

This deal points towards a shift: the smallest homegrown names are increasingly cropping up on the biggest conglomerates’ radars. At the same time, merit, clinical backing, and credible expertise are becoming deal-breakers.

As for The Func. Lab, which already cracked quick-commerce distribution and online and offline collaborations with lifestyle influencers and cafés for its hero protein powders, it now aspires to cement a similar positioning for its hydration-first supplements. The brand claims to deploy all the cash flow to accelerate product innovation, strengthen supply chain management, and multiply omnichannel distribution and presence in the time to come.

posts.caption:
India talks about caring for ageing parents as culture. But the systems around that care still carry shame, suspicion and serious gaps.
An old age home in India invites judgement. A senior citizen caretaker raises questions of trust. Apps promise independence, even as many older Indians remain vulnerable to scams, isolation and anxiety.
As families become smaller and more dispersed, elderly care in India is entering unfamiliar territory. Senior citizen care in India still revolves around duty, while the reality of ageing demands more: better healthcare for senior citizens, stronger support systems, technology designed for people who did not grow up online and, most of all, dignity.
Ideally, the gap can be filled with tech. But the only tech that can help the elderly gain some independence in India is one that detects when something is amiss and sends help automatically.
Read more at the link in bio.
By Karishma Kuenzang (@kkuenzang)
#AgeingIndia #ElderlyCare #AgeTech #IndianFamilies #SeniorLiving

posts.caption:
Everyone has taste now, allegedly.
A slick bun. A smoke cocktail. A microcement wall. A padel outfit worn nowhere near a court.
Instagram aesthetic has made everyone do the same things differently enough to call it personal taste.
This is tasteslop: aesthetic signals stripped of context, repeated until they feel hollow. The term was coined by cultural theorist Emily Segal for a world where good taste has become easier to copy than understand.
Artificial intelligence has only made that repetition faster.
So what happens when everyone has learnt the same language of good taste?
Read more at the link in bio.
By Karishma Kuenzang (@kkuenzang)
#InstagramAesthetic #Tasteslop #AlgorithmicTaste #CulturalCapital #Originality

posts.caption:
Motherhood is moving later because women have had to make room for everything else first: work, money, independence, partnership, choice.
IVF, egg freezing and better fertility care have made delayed parenthood more possible. Biology, however, has not adjusted its timeline.
For many women having children in their late 30s and early 40s, postpartum recovery can sit uncomfortably close to perimenopause. Fatigue, anxiety, sleep disruption and brain fog can start to look like motherhood, ageing, or both.
That is the blind spot. Women are expected to absorb hormonal change without always having the language, diagnosis or care to name what is happening.
The conversation around women’s health has to catch up with the reality of women’s lives.
Read more at the link in bio.
By Ria Bhatia (@riabbhatia)

posts.caption:
Summer has always shaped life in India. What is changing is the extent to which life now has to be organised around heat.
People are leaving work earlier, staying out later, sleeping on floors, changing their diets, adjusting their school attendance, and spending more of their income on cooling.
These decisions are often framed as adaptation, yet many emerge from necessity rather than choice. For those who work outdoors, miss a day’s wage if they stay home, or cannot afford air conditioning, there is little room to negotiate with rising temperatures.
Heat leaves few dramatic images behind. It does not wash away roads or flatten buildings. Its effects accumulate in the body, in lost income, disrupted sleep, worsening health and shrinking margins for recovery.
As hotter days are followed by hotter nights, how long can individual households keep absorbing a crisis they did not create?
Read more at the link in bio.
By Arshia Dhar (@urseaa)
#HeatwaveIndia #ClimateCrisis #ExtremeHeat #ClimateChange #SummerInIndia
posts.alt_text:
Until sometime back, beauty was the only goldmine for investor funding and acquisitions. Recently, wellness has been giving some steep competition.

Supplement brand The Func. Lab (@thefunclab), co-founded by fitness instructor Sohrab Khushrushah (@sohrabk82), cricketer-turned-entrepreneur Sahil Kukreja (@sahilkukreja99), and luxury brand builder Daneesh Davar (@daneeshd) in 2025, becomes the latest name on the block to raise a substantial investment within a year of beginning operations.

In its seed round, the nutrition start-up has successfully secured an amount of $1.5 million, powered by a group of well-established names such as Nisaba Godrej, Anand Piramal, Bhakti Modi, and Abhishek Nayar, among others.

This deal points towards a shift: the smallest homegrown names are increasingly cropping up on the biggest conglomerates’ radars. At the same time, merit, clinical backing, and credible expertise are becoming deal-breakers.

As for The Func. Lab, which already cracked quick-commerce distribution and online and offline collaborations with lifestyle influencers and cafés for its hero protein powders, it now aspires to cement a similar positioning for its hydration-first supplements. The brand claims to deploy all the cash flow to accelerate product innovation, strengthen supply chain management, and multiply omnichannel distribution and presence in the time to come.

posts.caption:
JACQUEMUS WINS FASHION AGAIN!!! For Spring/Summer 2027, @simon_porte_jacquemus returned home. Titled Le Bonheur (“Happiness”), the coed collection was presented at the historic Phare de la Pietra and served as a deeply personal tribute to the childhood summers he spent in Corsica with his late mother.
The collection balanced sun-faded romance with the house’s signature sculptural tailoring, reminding everyone why Jacquemus remains one of fashion’s greatest storytellers. Every season feels like another chapter in a life rather than just another collection.
I’ve said it before and I’ll say it again: nobody builds a world quite like @jacquemus.
#Jacquemus #SimonPorteJacquemus #LeBonheur

It’s going to be a good week.

posts.caption:
@dualipa is opening a library. That’s the headline.
Launching June 27 in Porto, the Manifesto Library will live inside Livraria Lello, featuring 100 titles that challenge power, censorship, and dominant narratives—from The Handmaid’s Tale to Reginald Dwayne Betts’ Felon.
It’s a natural extension of @service95, Lipa’s editorial platform, where she’s built a reputation not just as a reader, but as a serious literary interlocutor—interviewing writers like Olga Tokarczuk and showing up in spaces most celebrities wouldn’t think to enter, from prize stages to prison book clubs.
Call it unexpected, or call it intentional. While most celebrity “side projects” orbit vanity, Lipa certainly knows how to use her platform for the best of the best.
#DuaLipa #Service95

posts.caption:
The fashion industry has always traded in the currency of spectacle, but our current era has birthed a new sub-genre: mindless provocation masquerading as marketing genius. Yesterday, internet personality and “looksmaxxer” Clavicular (Braden Peters) opened the 424 Men’s SS27 runway show at Paris Fashion Week, which marks his second major runway appearance after his controversial debut closing Elena Velez FW26 in New York.
The fashion industry’s ongoing flirtation with Clavicular in particular is a clear marker of a culture that has traded creative vision for algorithmic relevance. What do these stunts and others like them say about the state of the state of the broader industry?
Read the latest on Substack: The Citizen’s Poste | posteposteposte.com
Images: @424inc, Guillermo Andrade
#Substack #ParisFashionWeek

posts.caption:
Jonathan Anderson’s second SS menswear collection for Dior arrived in Paris amid a sweltering heat wave.
Part English eccentricity, part French couture, the collection felt like a cast of aristocratic partygoers stumbling home at sunrise. Anderson described the collection as a fantasy character: someone concerned with dressing up, going out, and embracing a certain theatricality. The references were layered: echoes of rock-star glamour, British boarding-school codes, and Dior’s own menswear history all filtered through Anderson’s distinctly intellectual lens.
A year into his Dior tenure, the biggest takeaway is that Anderson is no longer introducing himself.
And what do we think?
#Dior #JonathanAnderson #ParisFashionWeek #Menswear #DiorMen

posts.caption:
Naomi Osaka’s latest entrance proved that the biggest fashion moments are no longer limited to runways.
created with Nike, Hana Yagi and Marty Harper, her custom look blended Japanese heritage, craftsmanship and storytelling into a moment that reached far beyond tennis!
today’s most influential athletes are no longer just ambassadors for brands. they are cultural forces shaping how we see fashion, identity and luxury
#TFO #wimbledon #naomiosaka #nike
posts.alt_text:
if you thought men’s short shorts were just a summer fling, Louis Gabriel Nouchi would probably disagree.

his SS27 runway doubled down on higher hemlines, proving that the thigh is still having a major fashion moment. the collection really made a strong case for showing a little more leg.

safe to say… the era of oversized, knee-grazing shorts isn’t winning this one.

#TFO #LouisGabrielNouchi #parisfashionweek #menswear #ss27

if you’ve ever said, “I’m just not a colour person,” Marc Jacobs is here to change your mind.
for spring 2027, Marc Jacobs delivered show packed with electric colour combinations, glossy finishes, sheer layers and ultra-mini silhouettes, all balanced with the playful irreverence that’s made his collections so iconic.
and with all the colour happening, the collection feels sooo expensive and cool rather than feeling overwhelming, the bold palette feels surprisingly wearable, proving that colour can really be the way sometimes.

posts.caption:
Fatherhood is a new role for the actor who first got noticed for his performance in ‘Masaan’ (2015). In fact, it seems this off-camera Vicky Kaushal (@vickykaushal09) is perhaps closer to Deepak Chaudhary, the character he played in the film. A sensitive, ambitious young man coming to terms with the world around him. That character, Kaushal says, changed him. But not for the obvious reasons.
“Until then success, to me, was where you reached [in life]. It was never about where you started from,” he explains. “That character, starting from the crematorium ghat in Benaras and going on to work as an engineer in the Railways in Allahabad [now Prayagraj]... He had a far bigger life arc than I’ve covered in my life. Till today,” he says, tapping the arm rest for emphasis. “After that I started having so much empathy for those around me, especially those from far less privileged backgrounds, because I don’t know where they have started from.”
@travellerprasad in conversation with the Bollywood actor at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
Thirty minutes after this interview was supposed to begin, Vicky Kaushal’s (@vickykaushal09) tall, statuesque frame bursts in through the door at the Matrix office in Khar, Mumbai. “So sorry, bro,” he says, shaking this reporter’s hand with both of his. His day has been busy—his son’s visa was to be sorted, he couldn’t leave his wife alone, and there’s repair work going on in his building, he says, sounding like your average young husband and father with his daily household chores to take care of, not some Bollywood star with a platoon of staff to handle his every whim.
Since becoming a father, Kaushal says he’s come to understand and appreciate “a lot more” of the work his parents did in raising him. “It occurs to me at the most random moments that that [instilling the right values] is now my responsibility towards my son,” he says. “He is going to learn by observing how his parents are thinking, talking, and behaving. So, I’ve realised whatever you want to see reflected in him has to come in your actions rather than words.”
@travellerprasad in conversation with the Bollywood actor at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
"Ten minutes into watching Widow’s Bay, Apple TV’s (@appletv) buzzy new horror-comedy, a feeling began to come over both me and my partner, quite like the ominous fog over the titular fictional island town: had we finally found a show we could both enjoy together, in earnest?
We were wheezing one minute, you’re watching through our fingers the next, and genuinely rooting for all of the characters to not die horribly. Soon I wasn’t even reaching for the second screen in my hand, and when does that happen anymore?"
At the link in bio, @coarsematerial on why this show is just another great example of Apple TV’s decision to prize quality over quantity.

posts.caption:
"I’m a bit of a pizza snob. Indian experiments like paneer tikka pizza have always left me unimpressed. So, at Gourami (@gourami.india), the newest south Indian eatery in Mumbai, I resist even looking at the ‘Southern Pizza’ section on the menu to avoid disappointment. But I’m quickly forced to eat humble pie, when a perfect 10-inch thin-crust pizza base topped with fior di latte and sprinkled with milagai podi and with a generous drizzle of ghee arrives at my table. Instead of the usual tabasco, chilli flakes, and oregano, this one comes with more podi and a small katori of ghee on the side.
And this is where Gourami excels—there’s the occasional detour into fusion cooking and experimentation with form, but the flavours remain firmly rooted in traditional recipes from southern India. [I will confess, even the chicken ghee roast pizza, with the Mangalorean-style chilli-tamarind sauce made in-house, is on point]."
Read @delishdirection's full review of the restaurant at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
It’s a hot Saturday night in Mumbai when the mezzanine of AntiSocial(@antisocialoffline) in Lower Parel has transformed into a dressing room. Members of the House of Luna(@houseofluna___ )—the family mothered by Glorious Luna(@glorious_luna )—and their friends are getting ready shoulder to shoulder under bright lights and borrowed mirrors. Roughly two hours until showtime, when Luna and her chosen family will descend the staircase to perform their acts in front of a crowd of about 500 people, the room is in a state of glorious disarray.
Makeup kits are open on every available surface. Suitcases sit underneath them, spilling out flashes of satin, sequin, and colour. A plastic horse head leans against a wall, a gigantic apple prop waits in a box in the middle of the room, and a curling tong—strategically placed out of everyone’s way under a table—is heating up. It may sound chaotic, but everyone knows exactly what they’re doing and what belongs to whom, what needs to be passed across, held down, glued on, zipped up or found immediately.
At the link in bio, The Nod’s @chloechou13 attended the fourth edition of It Events (@officialitevents) It Ball, and got a front row seat to the prep.
Photography: Aditya Sinha (@adipiscor)

posts.caption: The Atlas Of Affluence, the definitive white paper on the Rise of Indian Luxury and what it means across categories, culture, craft and new models of aspirations. Launching today.
posts.alt_text:
Luxury grows through conversation as much as commerce.

The IFCCI Luxury Symposium has become a platform for industry leaders, policymakers and global maisons to shape the future of luxury in India through dialogue, advocacy and long-term collaboration. Read more about luxury and synergy in this column that first appeared in the Atlas of Affluence 2026.

The conversation continues tomorrow, as the IFCCI Luxury Committee, in partnership with Comité Colbert, hosts India’s 𝑵𝒆𝒘 𝑺𝒄𝒓𝒊𝒑𝒕 𝒐𝒏 𝑳𝒖𝒙𝒖𝒓𝒚, 𝑪𝒓𝒂𝒇𝒕 𝒂𝒏𝒅 𝑪𝒖𝒍𝒕𝒖𝒓𝒆 in Paris. posts.caption:
All the world’s a stage they say, but for the four-time Grand Slam champion, Naomi Osaka, it might as well be her fashion playground. On day one of the 2026 Wimbledon Championship in London, Osaka walked on to the court in a custom outfit akin to the ceremonial kimono by Tokyo-based designer Hana Yagi to the chants of “C’mon, queen” and loud cheers from the stands. All white and well-within the much-debated colour code of the oldest Grand Slam, the statement-making ensemble was embroidered with cranes and cherry blossoms, with wispy layers at the bottom and a dramatic trail with a bow. It was made by upcycling seven different textiles, including vintage kimonos and a ceremonial shiromuku wedding dress.
Completing her look with a traditional kanzashi hair ornament, Osaka not only hat tipped her Japanese heritage but also an iconic moment from her favourite movie Kill Bill. She channelled white-kimono-donning deadly assassin O Ren Ishii, played by actor Lucy Liu. The Nike performance dress underneath further invoked her roots. A nod to the kirigami art of paper cutting, it spotlighted dainty floral appliqués and a curved hem. Apart from winning the sartorial sweepstakes, she also won the match.
This is not the first time Osaka has served avant garde style lessons on court, recent examples being a jellyfish-like Robert Wun gown at the Australian Open in January, that alluded to her embracing motherhood, and a black corset gown by Kevin Germanier at the French Open worn in May. The coverage around her has mostly tilted towards decoding these fashion choices, bolstering her image outside of the game and building a brand that champions indie labels and sustainability, as her stylist Marty Harper has acknowledged. The jury might be out on whether these dramatic walk-outs distract from the seriousness of the sport. But for someone who is self-admittedly shy, has openly spoken about her mental health and the pressure of being in the public eye—Osaka assuredly uses fashion to express herself. She’s more than just an ace tennis player and we’re here to see it.
🖋️ @unnatisaini
@naomiosaka
#thevofashion #wimbledonfashion #wimbledon

posts.caption: You are absolutely right and I hope something is done around it. But I think it's part of a bigger conversation and an underlying truth that all of us Indians are always looking for a "better deal". And that holds priority over art,craft,design,originality and everything in between. If we started respecting that we would think twice before buying a piece which by the way most of us know is a copy. There is a saying - "Steal like an artist". But very few understand or do that here. Having said all this I think India has an amazing set of designers and it just gets better & better.
