posts.caption:
In the middle of a heatwave that French President Emmanuel Macron said had “no equivalent in history”, Louis Vuitton (LV) presented Pharrell Williams’s Spring-Summer 2027 show in Paris on June 23. Staged under the arch of a mesmerising eight-metre-high, 37-metre-wide artificial tidal wave, on a sand-covered runway at the Cité Internationale Universitaire de Paris, the collection instantly went viral. The spectacle was undeniable. So was the dissonance.
Even as fashion feeds were filled with LV’s ode to surf culture, Le Monde reported temperatures above 40°C across much of France. With falling groundwater levels and restrictions on garden watering, swimming pools and irrigation, citizens were being asked to conserve water, even as a luxury brand created the illusion of a wave for a 15-minute fashion show.
LV’s defence rests on the fact that the water, sourced from Eau de Paris, was returned to the sewer system through a closed-loop circuit. The sand would be repurposed at the University’s beach volleyball court and the event complied with heatwave regulations. Technically, perhaps, all boxes were ticked. But tone-deafness is not always a question of compliance, it is a matter of common sense. One might argue in favour of fashion’s need for theatre and mise-en-scene. But when does fantasy start looking less like imagination and more like insulation from reality?
The show, staged in the gardens of a student residence, restricted their access to their own space. That detail matters. The collective backlash is about public space, privilege, climate anxiety and the optics of abundance staged against the struggles of students.
Williams, who may well have delivered what critics are calling his strongest LV collection yet, framed water as “the great equaliser”. But in a heat-struck Paris, the wave inadvertently became a symbol of luxury’s faltering sense of timing. When climate crisis is no longer a distant warning but a daily weather report, fashion cannot hide behind the language of “closed-loop systems” and “repurposed materials” while staging spectacles that scream excess. Sustainability cannot be a technical footnote at the end of a press release.
🖋️ @kimidangor

I create patterns, tools and resources that help you sew with understanding.
My work focuses on how fabrics behave, how garments are constructed, and how sewing connects us to the history of what we wear.
Sewing Patterns
My patterns are designed for versatility, wearability and fun. posts.alt_text:
In 1896 a Victorian novelist named Samuel Butler published a book arguing the Odyssey was written by a young, unmarried woman from Sicily.

Because how could an iconic text like The Odyssey, which features an array of powerful, intellectual, cunning women, possibly have been written by a man? Never. 

So his natural assumption was that it simply had to be written by a woman. 

Swipe to learn more 👉🏼

Full essay on substack 🔗 link in bio

posts.caption:
Homer already wrote complex, powerful, multidimensional women.
The problem was never Homer, it was 2,500 years of men translating him.
In this session of the Readings Big Book Club, scholar Melissa Mueller’s argument really hit me: weaving and textiles in the Odyssey are a coded means for women to communicate with other women.
Athena plants the idea, Nausicaa acts on it and Queen Arete reads the message in a garment. Three women having one private conversation. No man involved or even aware it’s happening.
Penelope unweaves her shroud every night and weaponises the suitors’ ignorance of women’s work. And in Book 23, Homer gives the grand epic simile to Penelope. He compares her relief to a shipwrecked sailor reaching shore. Her twenty years were equal to that of her husbands, Odysseus.
Emily Wilson didn’t add a feminist lens, she just removed centuries of male editorial choices. She didn’t rewrite Homer, she read him more accurately.
Full essay on Substack 🔗 link in bio.
In partnership with @readingsbooks
Cover slide artwork is Victor John Robertson’s ‘Penelope and the Suitors’.

posts.caption:
In 1926 Vogue published a sketch of a Chanel dress and called it a Ford and that’s where the myth that Chanel invented the little black dress was born.
But four years earlier, in 1922, the house of Premet produced La Garçonne which was a simple black dress with a white collar and cuffs that Vogue called “the most celebrated single dress the world ever knew.” Fashion historian Valerie Steele documented a million copies sold globally.
The designer was almost certainly Charlotte Revyl. Head designer at Premet from 1918 to 1930. Running a house founded by a woman and led throughout its entire history by women.
At the same time, British designer Edward Molyneux was making his own simple black dresses.
Chanel got the credit because Chanel got the magazine call-out. And where Chanel survived, Premet filed bankruptcy in 1932 and Charlotte’s name disappeared with it.
This is kinda how women’s fashion history works. The innovation happens, then only one name gets attached to it and all the other names disappear.
Charlotte Revyl and Edward Molyneux deserve their flowers on this one too.

posts.caption:
"When I create an environment, I am actually opening a field of experience that people enter in order to feel something. That is why I relate to the figure of the artist as someone between 'a nun and an activist,' someone who believes in intuition but also in the possibility that art can have an effect."
Amid a whirlwind season of performances and exhibitions, artist @ivana.ivana.ivana sat down with her longtime friend and fellow artist @sasatkacenko. Looking back on the works that have shaped her practice, they spoke about the stories behind them, a freedom that is never a given but must be won again and again, and the inner spark that keeps art in motion. Read the full interview in the June Issue of Vogue Adria.
Photo: @darqou

From quiet luxury-leaning manicures to full-on bedazzling, we’re seeing it all, but there's one style that’s cutting through all the noise (and has Taylor Swift's stamp of approval): Sky blue nails. After all, it’s a shade inspired by the joy of being outside during warmer weather. At the link in our bio, see some of our favorite sky blue nail moments. Photo: @craftedbyaprince

posts.caption: While we wait for the sophomore season to premiere, "Off Campus" star Ella Bright sported the summer's hottest upgrade to the bob: a clavi-cut, courtesy of hairstylist @JacobRozenberg. While the style usually hits right around your collarbone, this summer we’re seeing the shape sit a few inches longer with light layers and face-framing pieces. Want to refresh your look for the summer months both chicly and sensibly? Tap the link in our bio to see how.

posts.caption: America is celebrating its 250th anniversary this week. In the lead-up to Independence Day, @AmyFrancombe_ examines how Brand America is performing abroad. Once the ultimate cultural exporter — from Hollywood and the Disney Channel, to Abercrombie & Fitch and Starbucks — it might seem like the US has lost some of its international appeal. We unpack the US identity crisis at the link in bio.
posts.alt_text: @A24 didn't become a $3.5 billion studio by following Hollywood's rules. It got there by consistently spotting where culture was headed before everyone else — reshaping how films are made, marketed, and experienced. Now, it’s turning its hand to AI, and experts say fashion should take note. Tech editor @AmyEOBrien weighs in at the link in bio.

posts.caption: “Take note?” This is all planned… from the very beginning, from the “The One Percent” show during Paris Fashion Week with the participation of billionaire Bryan Johnson, to Mark Zuckerberg’s front row seat at Milan Fashion Week, sitting in the coveted front row of Prada, the same Prada whose runways championed social and feminist values. The AI ​​push seems more like a coercive market acceleration than a genuine expression of art and creativity for societies… those checks and quotas were already discussed behind closed doors, my loves 😅✌️

posts.caption:
Fashion loves to sell the idea of summer. But what happens when extreme heat becomes the norm? Sweltering temperatures disrupted men’s fashion weeks in Milan and Paris, while an extreme heat event during London Climate Action Week was cancelled, ironically, due to extreme heat. As consumers’ shopping habits shift with a changing climate, brands are beginning to invest in fabrics and finishes that go beyond surface-level cooling.
Can fashion adapt quick enough to meet the moment? @SophieBenson explores how brands are responding at the link in bio.
Photo 1 and 3: @styledumonde

posts.caption:
Despite the scorching heat, this menswear season in Paris offered standout moments from both big brands and fresh talents. The extreme temperatures forced some of the biggest names to reschedule their shows or move to different venues, while others unveiled set designs that seemed to anticipate the heatwave, despite having been planned long before it arrived. At the link in bio, @LaureGuilbault and @HilaryMilnes share the key takeaways to note from #PFW Men’s #SS27.
Photo @styledumonde

posts.caption:
The United States is back in fashion — at least, as far as luxury is concerned. With the US market on the up, brands across the board are expanding their flagship boutiques and venturing further into the country after years of prioritizing slim slices of the coasts. But where is the best place to show up? At the link in bio, @MaddieeSchulz breaks down the vibes, consumer profiles, and luxury hubs to know, beyond New York and LA.
Illustrations: Veronica Dall’Antonia

posts.caption:
For their first Vogue India cover together, Padmanabh Singh (@pachojaipur) and Gauravi Kumari (@gauravikumari) turn a day in Singapore into a sibling sport. Born one year apart, they grew up like twins with very different operating systems. Gauravi is “the planner, the pseudo-mom”, while Padmanabh continues to baffle her with spontaneous travel plans and a living-out-of-a-suitcase lifestyle.
Read the July-August cover story at the link in bio.
Head of Editorial Content: Rochelle Pinto (@rochelle.pinto)
Photographer: Zantz Han (@zantz)
Stylist: Manglien Gangte (@manglien)
Words by: Lamiya Chitalwalla (@lamiyachitalwalla)
Hair: Zhou Aiyi (@zhouaiyimua)
Makeup: Victoria Hwang (@vichwangmua)
Bookings Editor: Aliza Fatma (@alizaafatmaa)
Entertainment Director: Megha Mehta (@magzmehta)
Producer: Emily Teng (@eemileet)
Location Courtesy: Singapore Tourism Board (@visit_singapore)
Hospitality Partner: Frasers House, a Luxury Collection Hotel, Singapore (@frasershousesingapore)
On Gauravi: Dress, MAISON MARGIELA (@maisonmargiela). Clash de Cartier earrings, Long Clash de Cartier necklace, Juste un Clou bracelet, Juste un Clou classic bracelet, Juste un Clou ring, all CARTIER (@cartier).
On Padmanabh: Jacket, trousers; both HERMÈS (@hermes). Panthère de Cartier watch, Clash de Cartier bracelet; both CARTIER (@cartier).

posts.caption:
Rochelle Pinto (@rochelle.pinto), the Head of Editorial Content at Vogue India, travelled on Singapore Airlines to Queensland recently. The spacious cabins and luxurious seats that transform into lie-flat beds on board alleviated the exhaustion of all the travelling, she says. The state-of-the-art entertainment systems and amenity kits added to the experience. The culinary offerings include options like Book The Cook, where you can pre-order restaurant-style meals and also choose from an extensive wine and champagne selection.
Fly @singaporeair to Cairns from India via Singapore.
@queensland @singaporeair
#FlySQ #Sponsored

posts.caption:
Chaos is something @GracieAbrams has grown accustomed to. Eight years ago, she was a first-year student at Barnard with a cult fandom she’d fostered through Instagram; soon she was signed to Interscope, then on her first tour, then opening for Olivia Rodrigo and Taylor Swift. By the end of 2024, Abrams was a Grammy-nominated pop star filling arenas.
She finally took a breather last summer. “It’s not until you stop that you realize ‘Oh, I haven’t seen my friends in a long time. I haven’t cooked a meal in a long time,’” Abrams says. “The sleep schedule becomes regular for the first time in, like, four years.”
Now 26, she’s shifting into a new gear. Abrams’s third studio album, “Daughter from Hell,” out in July, sounds like her—earnest, introspective, folk-informed—but the production is more lush (“It feels like me in progress,” she says); she’s also in a happy relationship and making her first film, “Please,” with the filmmaker Halina Reijn.
For Vogue’s Summer 2026 issue, Brittany Spanos meets Gracie Abrams in Los Angeles and New York to talk about growing up, slowing down, falling in love, and where she’d like to go next. Tap the link in our bio to read the full profile.
Photographed by @larissahofmann, Written by @brittanyspanos, Styled by @jordenbickham, Hair by @tamastuzes, Makeup by @emikaneko, Manicure by @1.800.nailme, Tailoring by Irina Tshartaryan for Susie’s Custom Designs, Produced by @hyperion.la, Set Design by  @patienceharding. Shot on location @lucasmuseum. Vogue, Summer 2026.
posts.alt_text: This weekend, designer Michael Rider debuted his spring 2027 menswear show for @Celine in Paris—and what a show it was. Equally as buzzy as his lineup of modern-cool pieces was the front row that took it all in. It was filled with icons such as Grace Jones, BTS’s Kim Taehyung, Oscar Isaac, and legendary theater critic and New Yorker writer @Hilton.Als, among others. At the link in our bio, Als gives Vogue his exclusive recap on the collection.

posts.caption: A strange mix of celebration and solemnity is intrinsic to New York City Pride. Somehow, signs that say “Buy Tickets to Oh, Mary Now!” make sense next to signs that say “Love Us While We Are Still Here.” The march is also a space where Bowen Yang can joke about the “subtlety” of his bedazzled rainbow Grand Marshal sash, and 20 minutes later burst into tears upon seeing the signs made by the Asian American Legal Defense and Education Fund in his honor. See more from the audacity, resolve, and genuine fun of this honored queer tradition with words from @AdamEli at the link in bio. Photographed by @hunterabrams

posts.caption: Womenswear has become a playground for main character shoes. Perhaps no shoe has garnered as much attention as Chanel’s non-shoe from Resort 2027. But now, the men are beginning to see the appeal of a main-character shoe: an instant outfit elevator. The spring 2027 menswear shows in Paris and Milan proved a playground for the designers’ wildest shoe dreams. From Saint Laurent’s transparent lace-ups to Comme des Garçons’ witchy boots, the boys went toe to toe with the girls on freaky shoes at spring 2027 men’s fashion week. Read more at the link in bio.
posts.alt_text: Model @akbarshamji took Vogue behind the scenes for a day in Corsica as he prepared to walk the Spring 2027 @Jacquemus show. Discover the full collection at the link in bio.

posts.caption: Glam teams in Paris for the spring 2027 menswear shows had their work cut out for them in the record-breaking heat: keeping hair frizz-free and skin luminous rather than sweaty. From black-out contact lenses and sculptural lashes by @daniel_s_makeup for @rickowensonline, to the hair looks which ran the gamut such as @MrChrisMcMillan's coiffed retro styles, oil-spill slicks, and rippling curls for @WillyChavarria—tap the link in our bio for the best beauty looks from this season in menswear from the City of Lights. Backstage photographs by @styledumonde
posts.alt_text: The dominating nail trend for Summer 2026 is still up for grabs. From quiet luxury-leaning manicures to full-on bedazzling, we’re seeing it all, but there's one style that’s cutting through all the noise (and has Taylor Swift's stamp of approval): Sky blue nails. After all, it’s a shade inspired by the joy of being outside during warmer weather. At the link in our bio, see some of our favorite sky blue nail moments. Photo: @craftedbyaprince

posts.caption: The men’s shows have wrapped in Europe, but @MarcJacobs was back in action with his spring 2027 collection at the New York Public Library. He gave us an assortment of bold color combinations and all sorts of shiny and sheer (and sometimes both!) separates. See more of the best behind-the-scenes moments from his latest ready-to-wear show at the link in bio. Photographed by @huyylluong
posts.alt_text: Model @akbarshamji took Vogue behind the scenes for a day in Corsica as he prepared to walk the Spring 2027 @Jacquemus show. Discover the full collection at the link in bio.

posts.caption: A short, sharp shock of a show from @MarcJacobs tonight at the New York Public Library. Just four minutes long, but full of ideas about how to wear color this fall (brightly! and in unexpected mixes!), how to layer jewels (obviously faux and in big pile-ups at the neck), and how to go short—like, really short—and bare (with opaque bodysuits and stockings). Don’t forget your matching, or better yet, make that clashing, lipstick! Tap the link in bio to see every look.
posts.alt_text: Model @akbarshamji took Vogue behind the scenes for a day in Corsica as he prepared to walk the Spring 2027 @Jacquemus show. Discover the full collection at the link in bio.

posts.caption: Paris was blazing, but fashion never takes a day off. As temperatures climbed past 100 degrees, the spring 2027 menswear shows carried on—and so did the street style. Guests embraced the heat in their best warm-weather looks. Tap the link in bio to see more of the standout style from outside this season’s shows. Photographed by @mrstreetpeeper
posts.alt_text: Model @akbarshamji took Vogue behind the scenes for a day in Corsica as he prepared to walk the Spring 2027 @Jacquemus show. Discover the full collection at the link in bio.

posts.caption: The Island of Beauty. Today, Simon Porte @Jacquemus brought his signature Mediterranean minimalism to Corsica. Swipe for more from the scene, then tap the link in bio to see every look.

posts.caption: Men’s fashion week might be taking place during a heatwave this year—both Milan and Paris are seeing record-high temperatures—but that doesn’t seem to be affecting the street style scene one bit. As usual, the attendees are bringing their summer best. Tap the link in our bio for the best street style highlights from men’s week, with outfit ideas on how to recreate each.
posts.alt_text: For a city slicker’s take on warm-weather dressing that’s both practical and chic, look no further than our editors’ style guide. Global executive shopping director @libbyjanepage stays true to her signature black-and-white palette in head-to-toe silk, global director of social Sam Sussman has mastered the art of dressing for chilly office AC while staying cool on the streets, and shopping director @_taliaabbas makes a compelling case for Bermuda shorts. Shop their looks at the link in bio. Photographed by @theochoi_

posts.caption: “The first gift I gave my now-husband when we started dating was a carry-on in heavy linen with leather straps. He still carries it today,” says Dorsey founder Meg Strachan (@meganalidastrachan). A love for suitcases and glamorous travel style is not a requirement for the frequent flyer—though it certainly makes everything a whole lot easier. A staple duffel here, an outfit formula there… packing becomes less a chore, more a fun ritual. We caught up with the founder and creative director right before she headed to the airport for a trip to Corsica. Tap the link in our bio to shop her favorite suitcase (of the moment, at least) and see how she perfects seaside style.

posts.caption:
@zaralarsson is flooding all our feeds. Be it the soaring success of her fifth studio album #MidnightSun or the way in which #PinkPantheress's 'Stateside', featuring Larsson, took off at the Winter Olympics after #AlysaLiu skated to their track on the ice, there's been an undeniable, star-like quality to Larsson, who has been blazing through her world tour stops as of late.
And she's not about to let the dust settle—as she meets us on the cover of @voguesingapore's 'Embody' issue as one of two cover stars. "Performing live has been quite emotional and very rewarding. You can look at numbers online, but when a room gets bigger because more people want to come and dance and sing with you, that's something different."
In the link in bio, the electrifying pop star shares how her sound has always been inflected by her musical foundations in Sweden, connecting with her fans, and why she's never been able to imagine not doing music.
The #VogueSingapore June 'Embody' issue is now out on newsstands.

posts.caption: Leave it to @naomiosaka to once again, make a fashion statement on the court. An homage to her Japanese heritage, her Wimbledon 2026 entrance this time around was inspired by the traditional kimono. Designed by Japanese designer @_hanayagi, the draped look—done in tennis’ favourite hue—was made up of upcycled kimonos, as well as deconstructed shiromuku, the traditional ceremonial wedding dress and better paired with @official_mikimoto jewels. #VogueSingapore

posts.caption:
Following its selection for the Cannes Film Market and a win for Outstanding Lead Performance in a Canadian Film at the Toronto Film Critics Association Awards, #MontrealMyBeautiful (#MontréalMaBelle) is a film joining the growing body of works expanding the image of Asian identity outside the more familiar framing—that of recent Wasian meetup discourse, curated visibility, and tokenism. Instead, the focus falls on the lived nuances within the diaspora. Telling the coming out story of Feng Xia, a middle-aged, lesbian Chinese woman, the film’s most crucial proposition is its challenge to ageism: reaffirming the possibility of reinvention.
“I didn’t want to hide her desire. I wanted to showcase it, alongside her struggle, hesitation, fear and shame—everything together within her,” says @hexiaodan2025, the film’s director and writer.
In conversation with #VogueSingapore, He discusses using language as a means of expanding our view of the world, the immigrant identity, and spotlighting middle-aged queer narratives.

posts.caption: Nothing like a @jacquemus summer. On the island of L’Île-Rousse in Corsica, the sun-soaked Phare de la Pietrawas where @simon_porte_jacquemus revealed "Le Bonheur", the spring 2027 collection which arrives in dreamy splashes of colour, ruched bubble skirts and sheer slips that teemed with beachside ease. Touches of beautiful craft burst through at times, as a front row of @bn_sj2013, @meretmanon, and @isabelle.huppert and @alexandramalenaleclerc all watched on. #VogueSingapore

posts.caption:
Approaching wellness with intention requires knowledge. Our panels—styled by the glam team at @TheVIVAGroupSg—brought together leaders across fields. From traditional Asian remedies moderated by @NadyaHutagulang, and joined by @PhyVictoriaTanWy, Candice Goh (@SolHouse.Co), and @Chee_Hee_; to alternative fertility with @MargWang, Dr. Jessie Wai Leng Phoon, @iBea.Camacho—wellness was discovered from fresh perspectives.
Life should be well-lived at every stage. To that end, a conversation on longevity with @KathyGabriel__, @DianaKraemer_Wellness, @DrAndreaMaier, and moderated by @DrOliviaLesslar; to workplace burnout with @NicoleChew9, @ZayleaKua, and CrystalLimLange—the latter of whom led a masterclass on journaling with intention.
@SyfeSG covered all things financial wellbeing, leading a dedicated masterclass to passive investing; and a panel surrounding finances in an era of intelligent living—joined by the likes of #SyfeSG’s own Samantha Horton, Dr Catherine Ball, and Grant Wee.
Guests also discovered a curated selection of experiences that put self-care first. Think @Chloe’s summery scents; @Vetreska_Official treating our furry friends; and @CompanyOfWellness_SG and @WeeCheryl bringing their Signature Wonderchair Treatment for pelvic floor.
Innovation was reflected in every detail, down to the drinks—@IM8health’s nutrition-packed mocktail and @ThePurest.Co’s signature collagen peptide matcha. Meanwhile, @METTSingapore served up some glorious breakfast and lunch buffets—including the sweetest organic kiwis hailing from New Zealand to Singapore, courtesy of @ZespriSG.
Enlivened by @CopperGardenSG’s wondrous botanical arrangements and @Furniche.Co’s statement Bauhaus furnishing, #VogueWellnessDay was also about appreciating the simpler things—perhaps best encapsulated by the social dance hour that Snow Dance Studio put together, for a final ride out into the sunset.

posts.alt_text:
KAVITAON KA SILSILA AA RAHA HAI @sohotheatre LONDON. 💌🫀

They say everything iconic has once happened at SOHO (@sohotheatre ). The thought that Rajkumari will now be counted among the stories told on that stage still doesn’t feel real.

It’s strange… I can often find words for emotions people have buried deep within them, but when it comes to this show~my own, I always fall short.

Rajkumari has changed my life in ways I don’t think I’ll ever fully be able to put into poetry.

The last time we were in London, we promised we’d return bigger and better.

We kept that promise.

This is our biggest London show yet, and I’d love for you to be in the room with us, to laugh, cry, heal, and become a part of this chapter.

Tickets are in my bio. See you at Soho. 🔗🤍

@tarashna.management @beacon.live

posts.caption:
The quietest person in the room is usually the one who started it.
Daksh @itsme_daksh didn’t build WV Connect @wvconnect.in by being the loudest voice. He built it by being the most consistent one. Season after season, city after city, he showed up with the same calm, the same clarity, and somehow a bigger dream each time. Chennai. Mahabalipuram. Jaipur. And now Hyderabad.
The industry calls him the Face of South India. Not because he campaigned for it. But because he earned it the slow way. By caring about this community more than most people care about their own careers. By creating a space where a wedding planner from Coimbatore and a hotelier from Kolkata sit at the same table and leave as family. That’s not an event. That’s a personality. And it belongs entirely to him.
This season he brought the biggest dream yet to that table. Weddings meets Films. A conversation this industry was ready for but hadn’t had yet. Leave it to the calm one to start the loudest chapter.
The Founder is home. And this time, Hyderabad is the set. 🎬🧡
WV Connect Season 4 | Sep 1, 2 & 3, 2026 | Arth Resorts & Spa, Hyderabad
Venue Partner : @arthresorts
An Experience by @weddingvows.in
In Strategic Alliance with @designby_raja
#LightsCameraShaadi #WVConnect2026 #HyderabadZaroorAana
posts.alt_text:
Do Something 👆

Registrations are officially OPEN🧡 🎬

We could wait for you to “think about it”.. but we both know how that ends.

DM us. WhatsApp us. Call us. Text us. Smoke signals work too, honestly, we’re not picky.

Asia’s largest B2B wedding summit meets the film industry for the first time. 

Seats are moving fast.

posts.caption:
She doesn’t enter a room. She expands it.
Industrialist. Angel Investor. Former owner of the CBL Telugu team. Nandiniy Vijay @nandiniy_vijay has spent her career doing what most people only talk about — backing bold ideas before the market catches up. From the badminton courts to boardrooms across industries, she has moved with the conviction of someone who has never once waited for permission to lead.
At WV Connect, she brought that same energy. The kind that makes everyone around her sharper. Her instinct for spotting what’s next, her clarity on what actually works, and her refusal to settle for ordinary made our boardroom a genuinely better place to be.
When we talked about bringing Weddings and Films together, she saw it immediately. Not just as a creative vision but as a cultural moment whose time had arrived.
The boardroom just got more powerful. And Hyderabad is about to feel it. 🎬🧡
WV Connect Season 4 | Sep 1, 2 & 3, 2026 | Arth Resorts & Spa, Hyderabad #LightsCameraShaadi #WVConnect2026 #HyderabadZaroorAana

posts.caption:
Most people in a room talk strategy. Sanjeev @sanjeev_korada lives it.
CEO of Glosel, Founder of PathScript @pathscript , three decades of navigating global markets and building businesses that actually work. He has sat across the table from MNCs, SMEs, and everyone in between, and helped them find their way through the fog. That’s not a skill you learn. That’s a lens you develop over a lifetime.
What he brought to the WV Connect boardroom wasn’t just business acumen. It was clarity. When the ideas were flying and the energy was everywhere, he was the one who helped us see the path forward. Grounded. Sharp. Always asking the right questions.
An industry full of creativity needed someone who understood structure. He is exactly that.
When we said Weddings meets Films this season, he saw it not just as a theme but as a business move whose time had come.
The strategist the boardroom needed. 🎬🧡
Venue Partner : @arthresorts
An Experience by @weddingvows.in
In Strategic Alliance with @designby_raja

posts.caption:
@camimendes is having one of the biggest years in her career so far. A major summer blockbuster, the second feature film from her production company, and wedding planning are all on the roster for 2026. This month, Mendes steps into the sprawling world of Masters of the Universe as the sword-slinging Teela alongside Nicholas Galitzine, Idris Elba, and Jared Leto.
The project is a stretch from her days as Veronica Lodge in #Riverdale, which catapulted her into mainstream recognition nearly a decade ago. Mendes makes movies for “the girls and the gays,” she states lovingly, but her star power is only going to get bigger and brighter with Masters. “I’ve never been a part of something of this scale,” she says of the project.
In our Now Playing Issue, presented by @bvlgari, we chat with the actress about reinventing herself in this era of her life. Read it all in the full interview at the link in our bio.
photographer: @gleesonpaulino
stylist: @laurenegg
creative direction: @arrrrmani
hair stylist: @laurenpalmersmith
makeup artist: @jentioseco
manicurist: @thuybnguyen
entertainment director: @jbake21
writer: @balencianas
producer: @_porcelainchaos @delafe_pr
special thanks to @blackwidowmustang & @goldlinebar
posts.alt_text:
There would be no Pope Heyward without @jonathandavissofficial. Much like his smart, careful, deeply moral Outer Banks character, Daviss knows what it feels like to carry the pressure of proving your worth and refusing to let yourself or anyone else down. “What really stood out to me about Pope in season 1 was this idea of expectations and pressure that gets put on a lot of young Black teens and teens in general,” he says. “There’s this pressure to succeed, and it was something I truly have been through.”

Daviss still thinks back to his early audition days before landing the life-changing role on OBX, when the constant need to prove himself as a young actor felt emotionally exhausting. His mindset then? “You only lose when you quit.”

Head to the link in our bio for the full download in our Now Playing Issue presented by @bvlgari, from what to expect from the fifth and final season of OBX to his upcoming portrayal of a young Snoop Dogg.

posts.caption:
@victoriamonet is trading her claws for butterfly wings. The three-time Grammy winner is the definition of a multi-hyphenate: a culinary school graduate, a mother, and, of course, a singer-songwriter with a nearly two-decade-spanning catalog to her name. Now, using her 37th birthday as a launching pad, Monét has cleared her mental space and scrubbed her socials to prepare for her next chapter—an album she’s dubbed her very own Thriller, a sonically surprising, genre-defying project that marks a softer evolution. “The jaguar exists within me always, but I’m feeling like a softer version of myself now,” she says.
After all, Monét’s critically acclaimed debut studio album Jaguar II was the ultimate learning lesson. “Once you have an accolade like that, people often look for a reason to discredit why you got it,” she explains. “I think—pending a Grammy happens again—I would do the opposite and bunker down in the studio.”
Step inside Monét’s world in our Now Playing Issue, presented by @bvlgari, at the link in our bio.

A major summer blockbuster, the second feature film from her production company, and wedding planning are all on the roster for 2026. This month, Mendes steps into the sprawling world of Masters of the Universe as the sword-slinging Teela alongside Nicholas Galitzine, Idris Elba, and Jared Leto. 

The project is a stretch from her days as Veronica Lodge in #Riverdale, which catapulted her into mainstream recognition nearly a decade ago. Mendes makes movies for “the girls and the gays,” she states lovingly, but her star power is only going to get bigger and brighter with Masters. “I’ve never been a part of something of this scale,” she says of the project. 

In our Now Playing Issue, presented by @bvlgari, we chat with the actress about reinventing herself in this era of her life. Read it all in the full interview at the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
What, like it’s hard? For Lexi Minetree, stepping into the iconic role of Elle Woods was pure kismet.
The 25-year-old actress brings a young Elle Woods to life in Amazon Prime Video's Legally Blonde prequel series, @elleonprime, which is set in 1995, long before she defied the odds at Harvard Law School, navigating the highs and lows of high school. “I know Legally Blonde is so important to people, and they're very precious with it, and they don't want anyone to mess it up in the legacy. But I think, particularly because she is so involved, people are going to be so happy with where we have taken Elle Woods,” Minetree tells us of working closely with Reese Witherspoon, who serves as an executive producer on the project.
We chat with Minetree about landing the role of a lifetime and what we can expect of season 2. Read the full interview at the link in our bio.
photos: @jonnymarlow

posts.caption:
Georgina Rodríguez (@georginagio) on Tuesday revealed the debut of her new direct-to-consumer, Dubai-based activewear line, Mimoa.
Launched by the 32-year-old model, reality TV star and fiancée of Cristiano Ronaldo, @mimoa_com has created everyday basics within the activewear and lifestyle category.
The initial collection features 27 styles for under 100 euros each (about $125) — which Mimoa said was designed to “make the premium activewear and lifestyle wear category more accessible to women.” Eventually, the brand will feature 91 different variations once all hues and lengths are rolled out; notably, the leggings will be offered in both regular and tall sizes.
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio
Report: @kanikatalwar
posts.alt_text:
On Monday night ahead of the July Fourth holiday weekend, @marcjacobs returned to the New York Public Library for his off-season fall runway show that was chock full of vibrant, clashing hues and short, short silhouettes galore. Talk about eye candy!

His show notes, titled “Gratitude, referenced the importance of learning how to recognize abundance no matter the circumstances.

“Creating, as an act of gratitude, is my truest form of self-expression,” Jacobs wrote. Similar to his prior runway show in February, the American designer credited those who have inspired his path. Here, he brought Junya Watanabe’s spring
‘96 sheer and slick nylon layers (shirts, pants, trompe l’oeil lingerie slip dresses, and more) to life in vibrant shades of pinks, blues, purples and contrasted them over bold, color-contrasting opaque tights and bodysuits — a nod to Bob Fosse’s “All That Jazz.” References of Prada spring 2007, his own Louis Vuitton spring 2009, Yves Saint Laurent spring ‘93 and Chanel ‘93 were also present in both color and silhouette while sweet mid-Aughts nostalgia came through chunky piles of necklaces and layered belts that adorned a majority of the tight lineup.

Read the full collection review at WWD.com — #linkinbio

Report: @elmercer
Photography by @vnina

posts.caption:
Omar Sy is taking on a new role - with Jaeger-LeCoultre.
The French actor is the watchmaker's latest
"friend of the house" and the star of the newest episode of its "The Hour Before" series, which explores the often-unseen moment that precedes a defining achievement.
"With his commitment to his craft, his quiet confidence and his distinctive and constantly evolving style, Omar Sy epitomizes our maison's values and aesthetics," said Jérôme Lambert, chief executive officer of Jaeger-LeCoultre.
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio
Report: @lilytempleton

posts.caption:
@imfinalho_, the powerhouse vocalist of K-pop group @ateez_official_, made his Paris Fashion Week debut front row at Ami this menswear season. The singer attended the show dressed in a button-up shirt with sleeves rolled up, white T-shirt and pants, and slouchy bag — and like many attendees, accessorized with a handheld fan in the front row amid the city’s heatwave. After the show, he celebrated with dinner at Le Lobby at The Peninsula Paris and took in the Eiffel Tower in an all-denim Ami look.⁠
⁠
Jongho takes WWD behind the scenes in Paris as he attends the Ami menswear show.⁠
⁠
Read and see more at WWD.com — #linkinbio⁠
⁠
Report: @ktauer
